Bought this Ukuele for my 13 year old son.  He loves it.  It is definitely not an instrument for someone looking for a long term commitment.  For beginners, and for the price it is a winner.

I absolutely KNOW that nylon strings are lousy, and these will not hold the pitch more than 4 minutes.  I like the book and the CD, as these are published by my favorites, ALFRED.  I will try to get better strings asap.

Please don't listen to these amateurs talking about how the strings wouldn't keep tune. They don't know anything about stringed instruments or nylon strings. You have to keep tuning it until the strings stretch. Like all instruments, the strings have to stretch, especially with nylons, so be patient. It only took me about half an hour. It clearly states STARTER pack, so obviously its not going to be a high quality ukulele, especially because of the price. These people just do not know how to read. It has a great sound, but the 'lightweight gig bag' definitely is lightweight. If you want real protection, I would recommend getting a hard case or something along the lines. it is a great buy though, considering the price. I highly recommend this product.

Awesome "teach yourself" starter pack for anyone who wants to learn to play the ukelele. Easy to follow steps and directions. A carrying case and extra strings are a definite bonus. I would recommend downloading a tuner app to your phone is you can, to help you tune. Besides that, everything is great!

My husband is a singing, whistling, happy kinda guy and I thought that since I can't go out and shop I would shop on Amazon! Well, I had no idea what to get him so I just went through everything and I found this little Ukelele. It is a great buy for what I paid for it, it stays tuned and has it's own case and book with instructionss and a CD! Perfect I thought.
Then it happened. I LOST MY HUSBAND! REALLY I DID. All he is doing is laughing, and playing, and practicing and getting better on it, and I can't hear the news!  What a revolting developement this is eh? Oh well, he is having a great time and thanks Amazon the way you gift wrapped it was wonderful and very nicely done. Job well done.

Sincerely,
Cheryl Burger
Nice product for the price.

This is a really nice little instrument.  My only gripe is the nylon strings.  Not that they are bad, only I prefer steel.  The manual and DVD and CD were good to have.  It has been many years since I played a Uke, so they were helpful in re-learning the chords.

If you are serious about playing the Uke, this may scare you away from doing it. This instrument goes out of tune after playing it for about 10 minutes, and since you SPEND about 10 MINUTES tuning it as a beginner, this is unfortunate. It isn't the worst thing in the world, but just buy a $60-$180 one and get a real instructor.
summary: I would buy a better one

reviewText: Really fun for beginners! I would recommend for any beginner or intermediate looking for quality and affordability! My child loves this!
i wish it came with a tuner because my ukulele really needs it. but for the price for a beginner ukulele player, its pretty nice :)
Item as described, easy instructions on teaching yourself, but disappointed that the carrying case had a tear in it upon arrival.
I bought this for my 87 year old father. He will be taking a ukulele class next semester in his Senior College class at UNLV.  It is a very Nice instrument and he was very pleased with it.
Arrived quick, great quality, came with instruction booklet and carrying case. Awesome value! Was a Christmas gift and the recipient LOVED IT.
This item arrived as expected and was in perfect condition, just as it was described. It's the perfect starter for my beginner daughter.
I ordered 2 of these for my son and grandson so that they could learn to make music together.  They love them and are having a great time learning together.

This product is very helpful but it does not give adequate materials nor does it supply you with the best of instrumental help. This is an instrumental instruction booklet that would be best if picked up at a young age where it can definitely be ascertainable. This product for a fourteen year old such as myself is more of training wheel activity before being able to become "good". I think that i would have preferred picking a single uke that is way better than the fake crap ukulele that this set provides. IMPORTANT NOTE: It is probably best for you to go to your local Guitar center or music store that does sell ukuleles because getting the feel for it and asking questions to their professional personnel rather than picking one that SEEMS good on a computer screen. but if you are a 3rd-5th grader perhaps this is your best bet.

Hope this helps,
-C

It is truly a starter kit. The ukulele is cheap as well as the case (which is actually a bag made of the same material as a drawstring backpack in the shape of the ukulele) and the strings, but this is great for someone who is just starting out in string instruments. It is a nice practice ukulele, which came in handy for me since a few days into owning this, I already have many finger nail marks on the face from trying to learn how to strum correctly. It is made of a plastic-esque wood The book is the best part of it as it is relatively thorough, though the songs used for examples are mostly folk songs which the younger generation will not know, and Gospel songs which I only know from growing up in the church. It gives a nice overview of music notation and the dvd compliments the book well. Expect to buy a new ukulele soon after this one. This one is good for if you know you are going to make mistakes. It sounds nice when you play it until you hear the real instrument. Buy this if the user has never used a string instrument; if the user has played banjo or especially guitar beforehand, buy the book separately and get a real ukulele.

Makes good noises, has good sound,is fun,for kids that like it,
I think it is a good idea that you get it if you want it
Product is fine.  My (31+) son "played" it about 5 different ways without even touching the strings?  It was a fun gift -- we'll see if he actually takes the time to learn to play it as intended...

My room mate made the claim "I can play anything with strings."

Two day latter thank you amazon prime I had this little Ukulele waiting for him.

So far he has in fact been able to play "everything with strings" however the little Uku does not hold up.

Most people are not likely playing Smoke on the Water with this instrument but if you do try the Uku may not be able to stay in tune.
summary: Goes out of tune with any level of serious playing.

reviewText: we tuned the ukulele but it didn't hold the sounds? I doubt that it will be useful. If it wasn't a gift I would send it back
summary: quality not as good as expected

reviewText: This is a nice quality instrument for a beginner.  They began to play it almost immediately.  Instructional booklet very helpful.
I am only giving this item a one star since the product was defective out of the box. I was having high hopes considering I want to learn how to play the Ukulele.
I gave this to Toys For Tots.  I hope someone enjoys it.  I hope some one enjoys it.  thank you
summary: I gave this to Toys For Tots

reviewText: Made a wonderful gift! My son loves it!! Good quality, not expensive and comes with everything you need to start.

Overall idea of the kit is nice, however, the quality is poor and for the same amount a nicer uke can be purchased.

When buying this product there were several reviews / review points that are key to pay attention too.
1 looks cheap. The product arrived and looked extremely cheap. In addition it had several cosmetic flaws and a couple of hairline cracks where the base connects to the top piece.
2 The strings it came with were horrible. It could not be tuned and would not play. I had ordered AQU-AQ-S strings based on other posts and if you buy this product highly recommend you do the same.
3 The case is extremely cheap, however works and the book is nice for a beginner (was helpful in putting the new strings on).

I gave this to my 11 year old son and when he saw it was a uke he was so excited. Then he tried to play and was very sad. With the new strings (which, I included in the box) he was able to play and is happy though thought other ones he had seen looked better.
Great product my kid is having a lot of fun with it. She is learning a lot. Will buy again.

I love this inexpensive complete starter pack. It has everything that a Uke beginner is looking for. The Ukulele itself sounds very good except that it needs to be tuned a lot. I read that it is normal for a newly stringed ukulele until the string has stretched to its limit. Right now I don't have to do much tuning and I enjoy strumming and doing some picking on the Uke. I recommend the Starter Pack to anyone looking for a good and affordable set.

Bought it for Christmas for my wife, If she likes it will consider something more expensive. I am not huge fan of this review sight. I said what I wanted to say and it asked for more words. Will these suffice? It seems hard to keep in tune though.

This could use a low-cost tuner, I purchased an inexpensive digital tuner and it works great with this.

The instructions don't mention it, but for people new to strings, these will stretch a LOT at first so it will almost always be out of tune.  I could see a beginner worried that it always sounds funny.  After a while the strings will settle in, but expect to use that digital tuner EVERY time you pick this up at first.

It would be nice if the song book included Hawai`ian songs.  This is, after all, a pretty Hawai`ian instrument.
I bought this as a Christmas gift for my daughter. She has not seen it yet, but I took it out of the box, and it is a beauty. So cute and easy to carry around. It is the perfect size!
It wasn't at all what I expected, the strings are very low quality. Very sorry I bought this item. The manual is fine, the ukelele is terrible.
Strings don't stay tuned. So u have to keep tuning it. But the book teaches you the cords so you can teach yourself how to play.

For the price its OK. It feels flimsy but for a starter it will do. I decided to go with this once for the price and because as one of many whims my daughter goes through I figured why not. If she continues with it I will upgrade, if not it was only $40.
summary: OK for the price, good starter.

reviewText: This kit is excellent for me, I am a total beginner  and picked up the first song in about 2 mins, I need Pratice but I understand how to play it. I have to tune every time I play the song the strings are nylon and they stretch,but I need to practice anyway so no big deal. The c string and e string are staying in tune after about an hour and the rest are getting better. I did recommend this to a friend.

I purchased this for my toddler who is a fan of music and instruments... Well, my husband is now learning and so am I. We have a great time playing rock band and I love my sons concerts. He's pretty rough on it, not jimmy Hendrix rough, but rough, and it's holding up. No broken strings etc... I do suggest getting the auto tuner. Highly recommend.
When I got it the strings were so loose I could pull it about a inch off the fret, but otherwise it sounds great!
I'm not sure if this is normal for a ukelele, but it goes out of tune every time I try to play it.  Also feels very light and flimsy.
Awesome starting kit for uke players. Its not perfect because of the clanking sound it makes but it is a cheap so you get what you paid for. All in all this can get the beginners into playing.

I bought this as a gift for my Dad for his birthday, but he said it was unplayable. He said it wouldn't keep in tune at all. We are returning it. He liked it with the exception he was unable to play it.

You failed to send me the tuner for the Ukelele which is part of my order. Can you do something
about it. My son has been waiting for it.. thanks and God Bless!
summary: I've been telling you guys that I received my order (the Ukelele and carrying case, but without the tuner which my sons has been

reviewText: The pack is complete and okay but the ukelele has many scratches on the neck and looks like an used one.
summary: Overall good pack but the ukelele looks like an used one

reviewText: Great bundle.  Only thing missing was a pick or two.  I am still getting the hang of it and cannot play at all yet, but when I get more time to get at it I'm sure it'll work like a charm!

Perfect for my 7-year-old son who decided he wanted to take ukulele lessons.  The strings took about a month of re-tuning to stay in tune, but I understand that is typical for nylon strings.  The sound is very good and the size is perfect for a child.
summary: Great ukulele, perfect for playing around or learning to actually play

reviewText: I really can't understand how I got this for this price. The book it comes with is wonderful on it's own. I expected the ukulele to be more like a toy, but it's not. It's a cheap ukulele, yes. It's pretty decent though. My only issue is that it doesn't seem to stay in tune well at all. It has pretty good sound though and is easy to tune. With everything this starter comes with, I really can't give it anything other than a five.
Good value for starters. It doesn't stay in tune very well, but for the price being asked, still worth it.
summary: you get what you pay for

reviewText: Yes it does get out of tune easily but I recommend it as a cheap way to get started for a beginner. Later on if you do decide to get serious about it then buy a better make and model which will probably more expensive.

I bought this kit because I didn't want to spend a lot of money and I wanted to learn to play.  I figured if I really liked it I could buy a better ukulele.  After a few days of learning the chords and songs I was hooked!!!!  I am ordering a better instrument and going to buy more books.  This package was exactly what I was looking for.

I purchased this ukulele simply because it was cheap and came as a pack. I am new to stringed instruments but not music, having played clarinet and french horn all through middle and high school. I had read the reviews and expected it to not stay in tune for a while. Some people have written that the strings are a piece of crap, tuning pegs aren't good, ect. The one I received, while taking awhile for the original set of strings to start staying in tune, definitely suits my beginner needs. I haven't had any issues with any aspect of this instrument. I just want to stress that this is a beginners instrument, so don't expect it to be 100% perfect and clear in tone. It is cheaply made, so this uke will never sound like a professional one. The soft gig bag is on the cheap side, but it protects the ukelele, so not a big deal. All I can suggest with the strings is to tune, tune, tune, and tune some more! Stretch those strings out! You can either buy a tuner OR download one onto your smartphone. I have an Android phone and downloaded the app gStrings. It works wonderfully.
I can't wait to start learning how to play. This is a great starter kit for anyone who wants to learn how to play.

The sound is disappointing to begin with. I know this because I already have a Makala which is about the same price. Makala dolphin has very pretty and cheery sound as ukes should, but the Alfred has very dull sound.

Secondly, I tune all four strings perfectly, but when I play G chord, it's totally out of tune!!!!! I check the tuning again, they are fine! What it means is that the frets are not right!

First fault of dull sound is something I can live with, but playing an instrument that's out of tune is pure pain.

I give two stars instead of one, because it comes with CD, DVD, a book and extra set of strings, which is a good deal. But the main instrument is very disappointing.

This ukulele is a fun starter uke which I purchased four months ago, and it has been my obsession since then! I have gotten the opportunity to play on a few other different brands and types of ukes and am starting to know the difference in quality, but honestly I couldn't have asked for a better starting point.
My biggest complaint are the strings. I have yet to upgrade to a new set of strings, but they are really the only thing I have struggled with. They are nylon and tend to have a very muted sound. I did find I had to constantly tune the strings when I first got it, but I went through the process of (literally) stretching the strings which helped a lot. For the most part I can pick it up and not have it incredibly out of tune. The strings also seem to be a little thicker than Aquila or some other standard high quality strings and definitely leave my fingers burning after playing for awhile.
The accessories which came with it have barely been touched other than the case-and even that I upgraded when I traveled so the uke wouldn't get damaged. I used the book as a guide to get started but I always have trouble playing tunes that I am not familiar with so I ended up finding chord charts and songs elsewhere.
Otherwise, as I've mentioned, this instrument is a wonderful way to get hooked on playing ukulele. It's light and portable as most ukes are, easy to learn how to play from YouTube videos and other online resources and I played it all summer unlike my guitar which has been collecting dust. I even took it to Hawaii on a semi-spontaneous vacation!
summary: My first uke and I am hooked!

reviewText: the instruction book was nice but the ukulele its self is kinda crappy I don't suggest buying this if your looking to get it for the ukalele
my brother love it very much. he play it everyday. That is a real lukulele, not a toy. good stuff.
I chose for the pricing. Good place to start. Will move up as I progress. The quality of the instrument is sufficient for my needs. Thank You.
I used the Alfred Adult Piano Course so figured this would be just as good.  It is!  More experienced players have told me the uke that comes with the package is a good one for learning.

I purchased this for my sister who has now taught herself to play. She was a beginner Uke player but played both the guitar and piano well so she's not a beginner in instruments altogether. The ukulele is well made for beginners to intermediate players. It's pretty sturdy and the sound is good once tuned. It came with everything it was supposed to and was shipped very quickly. All in all, if you're looking for a Uke then this isa great purchase.
Used a few times and the bridge broke.  We had to glue and re-screw it in, but wouldn't stay tuned when playing.  Very poor quality, don't buy.

The Ukulele is certainly good enough to learn on, altho the book could do a better job of preparing you to tune it the first time. (It will take you a while to get the strings stretched, and prepare to keep tuning as you play the first time) and really, if you have no other musical instruments in your house you WILL need a tuner, so it's not quite "everything you need."  (I guess you could keep repeating that section of the DVD, but it goes REALLY fast) That said, there are apps for that, many for free, so if you've got a smart phone you are good to go.
I DO wish the songs in the book were a bit more mainstream.  I sing and have an interest in folk music, but some of these songs are so obscure even I don't know them well enough to know if it sounds right when I play.  The DVD and CD certainly help with this, but I don't really like having to fire up the TV just to practice.  I think if I were to do it again I would go with the Dolphin Ukulele as some other reviewers mentioned, with a different starter book.
summary: It's certainly adequate, but I wouldn't buy it without the CD/DVD.

reviewText: I liked the all-inclusiveness of this product, but the plastic strings and tuning keys were inferior and absolutely would not hold the uke in tune at all, not even for 15 seconds of strumming.

I play the uku a lot but this was terrible... The quality was bad, wood was chipping and had some weird white paint on it.... Everytime I tuned it and left it alone for 30mins or so it would automatically untune itself... Not worth it.

It's a cute ukelele, good for beginners and small children. However, the strings are nylon and they keep getting out of tune before I'm even done with a song! I was expecting the book and accompanying dvd and cd to be more helpful, but they weren't :( You'd be better off getting a Ukelele with better strings and a tuner, then looking up tutorials and tabs online. I ended up learning most of what I learned from youtube videos and ukelele tab sites.

Someone took the time to turn me on to ukes (although I had played a little in elementary school) at a music store. One was tuned and placed in my hands and I immediately named it. I couldn't put it down and I had to have one of my own. I was so happy, engrossed and transported. I couldn't afford to spend 200 on the one I fell in love with.  This is uke is great for me.  The book that came along is a reference as I use Youtube and some of the sites found here: [...] .  The only thing that i would change if i could would be better strings and maybe a guide on how to restring the uke.  Tuning will always be an issue if you have poor strings but if you are a beginner you should be tuning all the time to get your ear used to hearing the right notes.  I love the price because if i become disinterested I know i haven't invested too much.
Having a lot of fun. I need to tune it every time I get it out, but it tunes quickly. Tons of fun.

The ukulele is great if you are just learning the basics. If you decide not to continue playing then you didn't spend too much on it. But if you want to continue playing you might want to upgrade to a better uke. As far as the instruction book goes, it worked well for me. I particularly liked how it showed different strums you can use.

I play lots of instruments and wanted to learn the ukulele over the summer. This is great for little kids and for people who just want to learn the chords to sing along to. It's really cute and it's really easy to teach yourself how to play.
The fret board needed some work and luckily I have a friend who repairs string instruments. Not being a seasoned musician, I would have never known there was a problem.
The strings seem to fall out of tune very quickly. Once we get someone with a better ear to tune the Ukulele I think we'll be happier with it. My 10 year old daughter who we bought it for loves it.

When I received it, it looks like a toy. The quality is cheap n its all plastic.  I want a real ukulele but this product is s&^#! Don't buy it if u want to play ukulele. ..unless u want a toy. I immediately return it when I recieved it...

Case sucks cant really use it.
Came with ok strings goes out of tune A LOT even after a month of playing
good quality for the price, girlfriend and her daughter love it. I would recommend this sight to others. thanks for the great deal
book is good helps a lot with chords but uke is cheap and goes out of tune halfway though a song.

I bought this pack for my boyfriend but it say includes 6 things but in my package just arrived 3. the ukelele is so beautiful, but i didn't receibe the cd, DVD and the book. Im a little disapointment because i paid for a complete package and and don't receibe that.

Got this to get started playing the ukelele before I visited the Amazon rainforest. My boat crashed, and I was armed with only this uke to fend off a tribe of savage pygmies. However, I used it to wreck the tribe's chief in a musical battle of epic proportions and now I am their queen. 'Tis perfect for their small hands, and mine.
Get it, use it, live it, love it.
im a beginner, and the ukulele is very good, theres a book for beginners. I like it very much lol
This uke is cute, but it doesn't hold a tune.  I think I'll return it and try to get another one that stays in tune.
Ukulele is not the best one but it met my expectations, and the book is really helpful, you can find any chord you need easy.
summary: Alfred's Teach yourself to Play Ukulele

reviewText: The instructions for playing the chords are simple and clear. I recognize most of the songs and are easy to play.

I have never played a string instrument... and it looks like I'll be able to get the hang of this very quickly. Only problem is the strings. Sting one ... play's "dead" meaning it will not stay in-tune. Not sure if the knot is slipping or it is the string type that is the problem. Have another set of strings [better quality] and will try that set soon.
this is a great little Ukulele kit. has everything you need. Nice instructions. you will be singing and strumming in no time.
Bought this for my 3-year old daughter. She loves it.  It is light weight and small enough for her to handle.  Produces a good sound too
Very reasonably priced and the product arrived within the week. Sound quality reflects the price but it does give you an indication if you want to play this instrument or not.
summary: Alfred's teach yourself to play ukulele w starter pack

reviewText:
bought this for my son so he could hang around with his friends and learn how to play it.

he really enjoyed it till he sat on it and broke it.  Only problem he really had with it was tuning it.

This thing is so cheap, as in, it's made crappy!! I bought it for a fathers day present for my husband- He really wanted to have one for some family fun, maybe around the campfire... Although he does enjoy it, he has to re-tune it EVERY time he wants to play it. I would have rather spent more money on one that was made better and wasn't so cheaply made! I would NOT recommend this to anyone! VERY disappointed on this one! I wanted a wonderful Fathers day present for my husband and although the hubby said it was a good gift I know he was just saying it to be nice. I am really mad! My husband deserved an AWESOME Fathers day gift and instead, he got CRAP!

The ukulele itself sounds like a plastic toy, but I figured that would be the case going in.  As long as you know that's what you are paying for, it's fine.  I needed something cheep to try out for a few weeks so I could decide if I really would/ could learn to play the uke.  Once I figured out I was going to stick with it, I upgraded to a better uke.  The book contains your typical folk tunes that you would learn to play no matter what instrument you had decided to pick up.  If you have no musical background, the introductory section will be helpful.  I didn't get very far into the DVD, but I was grateful that the tuning section showed me that on my own I had tuned the whole instrument an octave too low.  Overall, the package is worth the $35.
summary: you get what you pay for

reviewText: I am having a blast learning to play this Ukulele!!  The instructional book is very easy to understand & the tutorial CDs help keep you on track.  By using the CDs, you can not only tune your Uke but it helps you to hear what it should sound like when played.  By doing so, you can hear how your progress is coming along by matching your playing with theirs.  I would suggest this for all ages!!

This produce was great.  The songbook was full of songs I had never heard before, but the instruction within was fine.  the uke seems great.  I would suggest purchasing the electronic tuner.  It was harder to tune than I figured.

the sound is also very good and crisp tuning problems of which I had read previously not found them, just a matter of a few days

refine it with piano or tuner and after the strings yield and there is no problem and can play many songs, the course is not very good I recommend buying a better ukulele without lessons.

the ukulele is very good quality I highly recommend it

This item caught my attention when I realized I've always wanted to play the guitar, and this was a close second. It was an affordable price, especially for the ukulele, the case, the book, and the CD. When it arrived in the mail it was in my arms 24/7. The book was extremely easy to learn by, and as far as I was concerned, the ukulele was a quality instrument. I now count the ukulele as one of the instruments that I can fluently play, along with the piano, saxophone, and flute. Overall it met my expectations by teaching me how to play the ukulele.
summary: I am now a Ukulele  player

reviewText:
The ukulele is suppose to come tuned and ready to play.
1)  It was not tuned...all the strings were loose and this had to be tuned before attempting to play.
2)  This did not come with any picks as advertised.
3)  There are two CD's one is just audio and has the same things as the other CD which is video/audio.  That is okay except that this is a soprano ukulele and the CD's are for a tenner ukulele!  Therefore, the CD's are pretty useless.
4)  Very cheap quality strings.  Had to go to a music store to get some decent strings.
These issues makes for a lot of frustration for a beginner.
Other than those problems, the ukulele itself appears to be solid and looks like the picture and if there were picks, CD's for a soprano and if the strings were better quality this would be a fantastic deal for the price.

My son is 15 and is loving the Ukulele.  This item is well made and
is with standing a beating.

Disappointed with the construction. The nut was glued on at a weird angle, not against the fret board. Don't know how this one got past quality control. It was more like a Chuckee Cheese prize than an instrument!

The ukulele is alright, it is not fancy in any way. But, it is a great place to start. However the learning software is a WASTE OF MONEY. After trying the software for a couple of hours it was clear i wasn't going to get anywhere, so i just went on YouTube and learned more in 30 minutes just by watching different videos.

The Pack itself was good---I liked the instruction book a lot---lots of training information, easy to understand.  However, I returned it all because the Ukulele was not very good quality, and in fact, could not get it to hold a tuner, even using the Snark SN-6 Ukulele Tuner.  The ukulele strings  just wouldn't stay put.  I plan to purchase another ukulele from a local guitar shop, but plan to also order the Instruction book that came with this particular pack from Amazon.  This book is really good and helpful if you want to learn to play ukulele.
summary: Alfred's Teach Yourself to Play Ukulele, Complete Starter Pack

reviewText:
this is a good starter kit for anyone who thinks they want to learn ukulele. it appears to be very complete and it won't empty
your pocket book
The Ukulele was just what I wanted. It came with a book and extra strings. I enjoy playing it. I would recommend this product.

I could see where if you were a "real" musician why this ukelele might not be for you, but as a first timer just picking it up for myself, it's perfect! It's not terrible quality and if I decide that I really am gonna be a bonifide uker, then yes I'll get a better one, but over all the construction isn't bad and the sound isn't awful either...I would suggest if you are a beginner, like me, that you purchase a tuner as well! well happy Ukeing!

Last Christmas, I purchased a starter set for my teenage son and after seeing it, my husband indicated that he wished he had one... So for Fathers Day... Surprise! It is a well assembled pack, perfect for learning and beyond. Great value!
summary: Purchased one for my son... And then...

reviewText: I gave this to my wife for her birthday.  She practices with the book every night.  The DVD is fine and the case is what you would expect.

I bought this for my daughter who wanted to learn to play. I got it only 2 days after I ordered it! Warning, You do have to tune it when it comes, But the book is extremely helpful. Now she can play things by memory(The first thing she wanted to do is learn "Im yours")
Highly recommend this ukulele.

I bought this for my husband who wanted to teach himself to play the Ukelele. As some of the other reviewers suggested, we bought a separate set of nylon strings and replaced them right away. The uke is great and the book had some basic tips and chords which are helpful. We haven't used the DVD. The internet is much easier.
Price is good!

This has been fantastic for teaching myself the ukulele.  I haven't gotten very far with it yet because of my schedule, but I love it.  Only problem I have is that it doesn't stay tuned, as other people have said, but this isn't a big deal for me.

It's good for a starter when it comes with a book and CD, so you don't have to spend too much time figuring ukulele out yourself. However, the ukulele itself isn't excellent; it's not sturdy as I  expected.

I loved this ukulele it's honestly great for those who wants to see weather or not the ukulele is for them.

UPDATE:
It's been three years and I still play on this ukulele and it still sounds great and works great. I bought this for $30 bucks and I thought it was worth it considering all of the things that it came alone with. I thought that by now I would have to buy a new ukulele but no it's still working amaizng! I haven't needed to change the strings and it's still looking good. To be honest I didn't ever use the case, or the CD's, or the book. So I just learned from youtube vidoes and also music sheets online. So if you're someone that knows they won't really use the music sheet or the CD then I suggest for you to look at other guitars that may be a better quality and spend your money there.
summary: UPDATE: 6/27 STILL AN AMAZING BEGINNER KIT. // An amazing beginner kit

reviewText:
Delivery was prompt as usual, but the product itself is garbage.

The fret board looked like someone painted it with a marker and it had an x marked on the right of the fret board to cover up a mistake or ding (probably painting the fret-board correctly in the first place would of eliminated that).  There was a very noticeable crack on the left side of the head stock (from keys to the tip). I spent an hour tuning it and wondering if it was the tuner, when I noticed as soon as I played it, it went out of tune. Actually it never tuned in the first place. It has a lot of mistake cover ups on the body of the ukulele so I am returning this for a refund. $35 is over priced for such a poorly made item.
This is a good starter for children to learn, and easy to follow with the song book and whole family love it.

reviewText: The whole package is indeed very cheap in price, but the ukulele is extremely cheap in quality. The fretboard is messed up so even when the strings are perfectly in tune, when pressed down, the notes are always slightly sharp so all the chords sound like a disaster. I don't know if it's just my specific ukulele, but I am not very happy with the overall product. You can do better, Alfred.

I bought this starter pack to learn the ukulele. I recently quit working and wanted to have a new skill to learn. The package arrived quickly and was packaged well. However, I had assumed that 'starter' pack would include all the items and information that you'd need to learn to play ukulele. I understand that you can play without a pick, but think it would be nice if one was included with the pack. I have been somewhat frustrated because from the start it was very hard to get the uke tuned. I don't have a piano, that would make it easy. I finally found info and a tuner app online. I don't see anywhere in the book that tells you should to tighten the screws on the back of the tuning pegs (I found that online). It apparently helps keeps the strings tight enough to hold the tune. I am not sure if the uke is still not totally in tune or if I should put new strings on, because when I practice, it still sounds out of tune. I think the book should be a bit more detailed. For example, a number of the songs are in the key of Bflat. Huh?  how do you play that? The chart towards the back of the book is confusing. I guess I should have done a little more research before buying a ukulele. I live in a small town that doesn't have a music store. The closest town with a music store is about 40 miles away. I'll have to take my ukulele down there and see if I can get some help.

This is a well made ukulele for the price....it came with excellent instructions and CD to make learning simple....I do not know anything about music and I have already become to understand and play this great little instrument....

This set was better than expected !!! The Ukulele is professional quality & the sound is really good. All the accessories that came with it was an added bonus. Cannot say enough good things about it ! Would highly recommend it to anyone wanting to learn to play !

I'm not sure if I am using it right or if it actually is a good uke so that is why it is hard for me to write about it. I bought this Ukulele because I wanted to pick up a new hobby.

summary: Thoughts on Alfred's Teach Yourself to Play Ukulele, Complete Starter Pack

reviewText: I received it earlier than stated.  It had everything it said that it was supposed to have.  The only suggestion I have is that they get the other Alfred's Teach Yourself to Play Ukelele.  This one is the condensed version that I had bought earlier and therefore, I actually use that one to learn the lessons on.  I thought the bag was going to be padded and it is not....It is like the thin clothes bags.  But again it is a great product.  Now to learn how to play!!

If your looking for a starter Uke and you want to learn, this is a good cheap way to start. Once the uke got tuned which takes about 2-3 days with the string stretch, it plays well and sounds good.
It's fit and finish isn't the best, and the sound is marginal, but overall it's a real decent value. Later when I have achieved more experience and talent, I can upgrade. I have paid so little for this setup, that it is easy for me to pass it along to someone else that wants to learn the Uke and there is no real financial loss.
Hint-Get the Snark SN-6 Ukulele Tuner. It is small and accurate, and makes Tuning a breeze.. which you will be doing a lot of the first few days. If it don't sound right to me, I just check it with the Snark. KEEP THIS FOR THE NEXT UPGRADE

You can put better sounding strings on this unit and it sounds better. But for learning the Uke.. it really can't be beat. I'd give it 5 stars if it were a better quality Uke... Plus it's a unit that can go on the road in a pack on a motorcycle, to the college dorm, and there is no worries your risking damage to an expensive unit. It's a perfect learning instrument. Then you can do with it what you want once your ready to upgrade... give it to a niece or nephew, grand child... send it off to college, you name it.. it will always be worth what you paid for it....
I have no negitives.. it is what it is... designed to be a starter Ukulele, and it preforms that task with class... I am not afraid to show or let pro's play it and look at it.. they are also impressed with its quality at the cost. I used lemon oil on it and hand rubbed it out to a dull luster, and put better strings on it.. so with that.. it is impressive for the cost.
There are better Uke's on the market... those are for pro's. This is a starter unit.
its all good, you see, you get the book, extra strings, carrying case. frist thing is how to tune the ukulele

While the item was delivered as promised, I must question the actual quality of the Firebrand Ukelele. The item I purchased has developed a crack along the tip of the neck after only a week of use. I can admit it was banged a bit by my sons, but nothing that can explain the damage. Actually upon close inspection the neck looks to be made of separate pieces edge glued. The stain patterns follow the exact arc the crack appeared on. I find this highly suspect.

I have contacted the seller by email and hope to hear back soon about a replacement, but until then I cannot give this items anything but one star.

Very disappointed right now.
The ukelele is made out of cheap wood and it feels awful. The strings are low quality and everything about the package screams out horrible. I am very happy Amazon has a return policy.
